wifiFirmware.notes: The original Pi Zero W carries a single WiFi/BT combo chip revision, plain BCM43430 (no b0), unlike the Zero 2 W which spans three revisions. In RPi-Distro/firmware-nonfree the brcmfmac driver's board+chip lookup for 'raspberrypi,model-zero-w' resolves via git symlinks to the SAME blob set the Pi 3 Model B uses ('raspberrypi,3-model-b'): brcmfmac43430-sdio.raspberrypi,model-zero-w.{bin,clm_blob} -> ../cypress/cyfmac43430-sdio.{bin,clm_blob} (a Cypress-branded blob living in a sibling 'cypress/' upstream directory, NOT the 'brcm/' directory the pi-zero-2w manifest pulled from), and brcmfmac43430-sdio.raspberrypi,model-zero-w.txt -> brcmfmac43430-sdio.txt (a real file alongside the symlinks in 'brcm/'). This differs from the Zero 2 W's plain-43430 alias, which resolves to brcmfmac43436s-sdio.* (a brcm/-native blob) rather than the cypress/ one -- the two boards' '43430' chip IDs are NOT interchangeable blobs despite the shared naming convention. No 43430b0 alias exists for 'raspberrypi,model-zero-w' in this repo (checked: only .../model-zero-2-w and .../3-model-b/0-compute-module have a b0 variant), confirming the original Zero W shipped a single WiFi module revision throughout production. Since gosd's initramfs can't carry symlinks (per gosd-eu2x), a board profile must materialize all three alias dest names as literal duplicate copies of their 'of' file's bytes, flattened into the SAME destDir ('brcm/') as the base files -- mirroring how gosd-3zrc wired the pi-zero-2w aliases -- even though upstream splits the real bytes across 'brcm/' and 'cypress/' directories.
